label_change.evidence: The source judgment is a plaintiff row and the substantive outcome for ABB Industry Pte Ltd is not separately stated. The judgment only states at [111] that judgment will be for the second and third plaintiffs against the defendant, while the first plaintiff's claim is dismissed. ABB Industry Pte Ltd is one of the plaintiffs, but the row does not clearly identify whether it is the second or third plaintiff from the metadata alone.
label_change.recommended_change: Change the label to Unknown because the row's party identity does not clearly map to a specific substantive outcome in the judgment.

fact_changes.evidence: The source text states that the defendant was based in Singapore from January 1997 and that he held positions with the second and third plaintiffs during that period. ABB Industry Pte Ltd is the third plaintiff, and the relevant role transfer to the third plaintiff took effect from 1 January 2001.
fact_changes.recommended_change: Replace the unsupported 1990 date with 2001-01-01, which is the earliest date explicitly tied to ABB Industry Pte Ltd in the source.

fact_changes.evidence: The source text says the first plaintiff is the holding company of the second and third plaintiffs, but it does not state that ABB Industry Pte Ltd was wholly owned by ABB Holdings Pte Ltd. It states only that the first plaintiff was owned by ABB Asea Brown Boveri Ltd and was the sole shareholder of the second and third plaintiffs.
fact_changes.recommended_change: Remove the unsupported ownership assertion or rewrite it to reflect only that ABB Holdings Pte Ltd is the first plaintiff and sole shareholder of the second and third plaintiffs.

fact_changes.evidence: The source text states that on 15 January 2001, pursuant to a new organisation structure effective from 1 January 2001, the defendant's employment was transferred from the second plaintiff to the third plaintiff with retrospective effect to 1 January 2001. It does not say he was General Manager of ABB Industry Pte Ltd from 1 January 2001.
fact_changes.recommended_change: Revise the fact to state that from 1 January 2001 the defendant's employment was transferred to the third plaintiff, ABB Industry Pte Ltd, and that he served there as General Manager BAU LV Products and Systems.

fact_changes.current_value: From 1 January 2001 to February 2003, Sher Hock Guan Charles held the position of General Manager of ABB Industry Pte Ltd.
fact_changes.recommended_value: With retrospective effect from 1 January 2001, Sher Hock Guan Charles's employment was transferred to ABB Industry Pte Ltd, where his designation was General Manager BAU LV Products and Systems.
fact_changes.evidence: Paragraph 17 of the source states that the third contract letter dated 15 January 2001 transferred his employment to the third plaintiff with retrospective effect from 1 January 2001 and designated him as 'General Manager BAU LV Products and Systems'.
fact_changes.recommended_change: Correct the title and avoid overstating the duration as a standalone general manager role.

fact_changes.evidence: The source text states that from 3 September 2001, the defendant also served as Vice-President and Head of Business Area Low Voltage Products, Automation Technology Products Division Asia South, third plaintiff.
fact_changes.recommended_change: Correct the start date to 3 September 2001.

fact_changes.current_value: With effect from 1 January 2001, ABB Industry Pte Ltd became the employing entity for Sher Hock Guan Charles following a reorganisation of the ABB Singapore Group.
fact_changes.recommended_value: With retrospective effect from 1 January 2001, Sher Hock Guan Charles's employment was transferred from the second plaintiff to ABB Industry Pte Ltd pursuant to a new organisation structure that came into effect on 1 January 2001.
fact_changes.evidence: Paragraph 2 of the source states that on 15 January 2001, pursuant to a new organisation structure for the ABB Singapore Group which had come into effect on 1 January 2001, his employment was transferred from the second plaintiff to the third plaintiff with retrospective effect to 1 January 2001.
fact_changes.recommended_change: Replace the simplified wording with the source's exact transfer description.

fact_changes.current_value: In February 2003, Sher Hock Guan Charles left the employment of ABB Industry Pte Ltd.
fact_changes.recommended_value: After leaving the third plaintiff, Sher Hock Guan Charles joined Huadian as General Manager; his appointment took effect on 3 March 2003.
fact_changes.evidence: The source says he left the ABB Group in February 2003 and that after leaving the third plaintiff he joined Huadian as General Manager, with that appointment taking effect on 3 March 2003.
fact_changes.recommended_change: Avoid stating an exact employment end date not given in the source; use the source's wording that he left the third plaintiff before joining Huadian.

fact_changes.evidence: The source only says 'In late October 2002' the defendant attended the 10th anniversary celebrations of ABB Xiamen. It does not give an exact day.

fact_changes.current_value: In late October 2002, while still employed by ABB Industry Pte Ltd, Sher Hock Guan Charles attended the 10th anniversary celebrations of ABB Xiamen Switchgear Co Ltd in Xiamen, China.

fact_changes.recommended_change: Use the source's wording and avoid adding a fuller company name not used in the relevant passage.

fact_changes.evidence: The source says 'During conversations with them the defendant learnt that XIHARI was looking for a technical adviser' at the late October 2002 event, but does not specify an exact date.

fact_changes.evidence: The source says 'In November 2002' officers of XIHARI discussed by telephone with the defendant their wish to engage an internationally known technical adviser and mentioned Mr Leonhardt's name.

fact_changes.evidence: The source states 'Between 3 December 2002 and 3 February 2003, nine e-mails were exchanged' and does not require the exact start date for a background fact row.
fact_changes.recommended_change: If retained, use the date range as stated in the source rather than converting it into a single exact date.

fact_changes.evidence: The source says 'In November 2002, Mr Pei and another officer of XIHARI ... mentioned in various telephone conversations with the defendant' and does not specify a day.

fact_changes.evidence: The source says 'By 31 January 2003, Huadian had finished prototyping and the most critical type testing.'

fact_changes.evidence: The source says that by the end of January 2003 the defendant had decided to leave the third plaintiff and had told a senior officer, Tom Sjoekvist.

fact_changes.current_value: On 3 March 2003, after leaving ABB Industry Pte Ltd, Sher Hock Guan Charles commenced employment as General Manager of Xiamen Huadian Switchgear Co. Ltd.

fact_changes.evidence: The source states that by 28 March 2004, the shareholders in Huadian were Fu Hsiang (80.5%), XIHARI (3.3%) and Gelpag mbH (16.2%). fact_changes.evidence: The source says 'On 5 or 6 March 2003' the defendant called Mr Leonhardt and invited him to visit Huadian's premises.
fact_changes.recommended_change: Use an approximate early-March 2003 date rather than a specific day.

fact_changes.evidence: The source says the consultancy agreement was signed in late March 2003 and backdated to 1 March 2003.

fact_changes.evidence: The source says Huadian commenced marketing its vacuum circuit breaker in July 2003 and received the first order in September 2003.

fact_changes.current_value: On 18 February 2002, Great Vision International Pte Ltd was incorporated in Singapore by Sher Hock Sing and two other individuals, and this company was used to undertake a property development project in Xiamen through a subsidiary, Xiamen Fu Cheng Real Estate Property Development Co. Ltd.

fact_changes.current_value: In February 2002, Sher Hock Guan Charles became aware of an opportunity to develop a plot of land in Xiamen and passed this opportunity to his brother, Sher Hock Sing, and business associate Lin Qunxing, leading to the incorporation of Great Vision International Pte Ltd to pursue the project.

fact_changes.current_value: On 3 December 2002, a payment voucher of Xiamen Fu Cheng Real Estate Property Development Co.
